skill.name: flutter-expert
skill.description: Use when building cross-platform Flutter 3 applications with Dart, Riverpod, Bloc, GoRouter, reusable widgets, project structure, and performance optimization.
skill.summary: Build Flutter 3 apps with Dart architecture, state management, navigation, widgets, and performance guidance.
skill.author: jeffallan

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, network, key, storage, reconnaissance, and weak-crypto patterns, but review found documentation-only Flutter and Dart examples. No code executes automatically, no secrets are accessed, and no prompt injection text was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The external command alerts point to Markdown code fences, inline reference paths, Dart snippets, and Flutter CLI documentation. These examples are inert skill content and do not run commands automatically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are Markdown fences or inline code references. The only shell commands are Flutter profiling examples in documentation, with no autonomous execution path.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Flutter Key Syntax Flagged as Credential Material
low_findings.description: The certificate and key alerts match Flutter widget constructor syntax such as super.key, Key, and route query key terms. No private keys, certificates, or credential files are present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Every cited occurrence is ordinary Flutter API syntax or documentation table text. There is no secret value, encoded material, or file path to credential artifacts.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Crypto Alerts Match Ordinary Words
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts match ordinary documentation text, including description, auth guidance, and desktop layout terms. No MD5, SHA1, DES, or cryptographic API use was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the static locations found natural language and UI layout identifiers. There is no implementation of cryptographic functions or insecure algorithm selection.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Alerts Match Dart Examples
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance alerts occur inside Flutter routing and state-management examples, such as route parameters, Todo methods, and widget callbacks. They do not inspect host system information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are application sample code, not shell commands or host probes. No uname, whoami, environment enumeration, or filesystem reconnaissance behavior appears.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Hardcoded URL Is Author Metadata
low_findings.description: The network alert is a GitHub author URL in skill metadata. It is not a runtime endpoint and no data is sent to it by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL appears only in frontmatter metadata as the author attribution. There is no fetch, HTTP client, webhook, or exfiltration path associated with it.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Storage References Are Architecture Guidance
low_findings.description: The browser storage alert matches Flutter dependency and architecture guidance for shared preferences, Hive, and data layer responsibilities. The skill itself does not access browser storage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines describe Flutter project structure and dependency choices. No executable code reads cookies, localStorage, sessionStorage, or browser credentials.

content.user_title: Build Flutter apps with expert patterns
content.value_statement: Flutter apps need consistent architecture, state management, navigation, and performance practices. This skill gives focused guidance for Flutter 3, Dart, Riverpod, Bloc, GoRouter, and profiling.
actual_capabilities: Design feature-based Flutter project structures with clear app, core, shared, routes, and feature layers.
Implement Riverpod providers, Notifiers, AsyncNotifiers, and selective rebuild patterns.
Build Bloc and Cubit flows with immutable state, listeners, builders, and tests.
Configure GoRouter routes, nested routes, redirects, shell routes, and query parameters.
Apply Flutter performance practices for const widgets, images, isolates, lists, and repaint boundaries.
limitations: Does not run, compile, or test Flutter projects by itself.
Does not provide backend API implementations or cloud infrastructure.
Does not replace platform-specific native iOS or Android expertise.
Guidance may need updates for newer Flutter, Dart, or package releases.

use_cases.title: Start a Flutter product
use_cases.description: Plan project structure, dependencies, routing, and state management before the first feature is built.

use_cases.title: Standardize team patterns
use_cases.description: Align widgets, Riverpod, Bloc, and GoRouter usage across a growing Flutter codebase.

use_cases.title: Improve app performance
use_cases.description: Identify rebuild, image, list, isolate, and repaint patterns that commonly cause Flutter jank.

prompt_templates.title: Create a simple widget
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a reusable Flutter card widget for a product item. Use const constructors, clear properties, and Material design conventions.

prompt_templates.title: Add Riverpod state
prompt_templates.prompt: Design Riverpod state for a todo list with loading, error, add, toggle, and remove behavior. Include widget usage guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Build guarded navigation
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up GoRouter for authenticated and unauthenticated areas. Include redirects, nested routes, shell navigation, and query parameter handling.

prompt_templates.title: Audit performance problems
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Flutter feature for performance risks. Focus on rebuilds, image loading, list rendering, isolates, and profiling steps.

output_examples.input: I need a Riverpod pattern for a profile screen with loading and update states.
output: A recommended provider structure for profile data and updates.
A screen flow that handles loading, error, empty, and success states.
Notes on selective watching to avoid unnecessary rebuilds.

output_examples.input: I need GoRouter guidance for login, tabs, and detail pages.
output: A route plan covering public, protected, tabbed, and detail destinations.
Redirect rules for signed-in and signed-out users.
Deep-linking considerations for path and query parameters.

output_examples.input: My Flutter list screen feels slow when scrolling.
output: A prioritized performance checklist for list building and image loading.
Suggestions for const widgets, repaint boundaries, and cached images.
Profiling steps to confirm improvements before and after changes.

best_practices: Choose Riverpod for reactive state and Bloc for explicit event-driven workflows.
Use const constructors, stable keys, and selective watching to reduce rebuild costs.
Profile with Flutter DevTools before changing performance-sensitive code.
anti_patterns: Using setState for app-wide or cross-feature state.
Building large widget trees inside one build method instead of extracting reusable widgets.
Adding performance fixes without measuring rebuilds, frame time, or memory use.

faq.question: What Flutter versions does this skill target?
faq.answer: It targets Flutter 3 and Dart 3 patterns, with references to Riverpod 2, Bloc 8, and GoRouter.

faq.question: Can it choose between Riverpod and Bloc?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains when each approach fits and how to structure providers, blocs, cubits, and widgets.

faq.question: Does it include navigation guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers GoRouter setup, redirects, nested routes, shell routes, path parameters, and query parameters.

faq.question: Can it help with performance issues?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers const widgets, selective rebuilds, image optimization, isolates, RepaintBoundary, and DevTools profiling.

faq.question: Does it generate complete production apps?
faq.answer: No. It provides implementation patterns and examples, but production delivery still needs integration, testing, and review.

faq.question: Is the skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. Review found documentation-only examples, no automatic command execution, no credential access, and no prompt injection.
